narrative.summary: Vietnamese-inflected French/Italian pastries; rotating menu; 4.8 rating. Tiny gem, no seating.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You'll walk past it on Bloor West near Christie Pits if you're not looking carefully—there's barely a sign. Inside, it's a takeaway counter operation with pastries arranged in the case and nothing else: no tables, no chairs, just a small room where you point at what you want and leave. The whole transaction takes two minutes, which is exactly the point.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The menu rotates, but the through-line is Vietnamese technique meeting French and Italian pastry traditions—think tomato focaccia that's airy and almost too soft, char siu bao with housemade filling, and coconut buns that sell out by mid-morning. You'll also find butter tarts (proper sweet ones), apple crumble pastries, and a shifting lineup of savory options that changes based on what they're feeling that week. Everything's under $10, and by 10:30 a.m. on weekends, half the case is already gone.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Go early on a Saturday morning when you want something better than a coffee shop croissant but don't want to sit anywhere. Grab a few things, walk to Christie Pits, and eat them on a bench before the park fills up.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Lovely little place tucked away without a big sign, which makes it feel like a hidden local gem. I found Bà Nội with the help of the address, 806 Bloor West.

The tomato focaccia was fluffy and delicious. The butter tart met expectations — sweet and good. By 10:30 a.m., the apple crumble pastries were almost sold out, other options on display included coconut buns, cookies, and a variety of savory pastries. Everything looked tempting and worth trying, like immediately :)"

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Hi.
Had the char Siu Bao and felt the ratio of bun to filling wasn't right as it was a little too much bun so some bites was more bread and less char Siu. And the the char Siu was plentiful but lacked a sweetness and fluidity to make every bite 'mouth watering '. Also the bread could benefit from a little more sweetness.  I'd rate this a 3.5

The curry bun was ok. And wish the curry was a little more kick/curry sauce and just gave us the 'wow effects ' when taking a bite.  I'd rate this a 3.5.

Butter tart is really good!  Crust was crispy and a tad thick (very picky here as I think a crust role is to hold the butter filling and not overwhelm the taste - personal thought but let's me clear.this is an excellent crust.) and filling was sweet, earthy almost burned sugar/ carsmel like.  Almost perfection if there was more filling to give every bite (and maybe I take top big of a bite) a buttery explosion in your mouth.

I look forward to returning and trying their other items.

Staff is so so friendly and they made this a very enjoyable and happy visit."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I don’t think I can say anything new that other reviews haven’t already said but to add to them, this place is super special - best butter tarts in Toronto if not Canada. They also do amazing cookies, curry beef buns, savour danishes, sourdoughs and coffee. I love getting a treat with Vietnamese coffee in the summer. But I visit all year round. Yes, their coffee is also amazing. Expect for there to be lineups at times depending on time of day or day of the week!"
